widgets_values:
Edit the provided image with a soft enhancement pass. Preserve the original image, composition, lighting, colors, and subject exactly as they are, and only refine the image subtly. Improve overall clarity, detail, sharpness, texture, and realism in a natural way, while keeping the result gentle and believable. Do not overprocess, oversharpen, or redesign anything. Keep the enhancement clean, light, and faithful to the original.

maintain make-up, piercings, or any other  visual markers.

nodes.title: 📐 Aspect Ratio + Scaled Resolution Table / 画面比例与放大分辨率表

nodes.title: warning
widgets_values: warnings gives you the heads up about what didn't work in the prompt generation or comflicts.

nodes.title: SUMMARY
widgets_values:
The summary gives you a detailed breakdown of how the prompt was generated and which classes or categories were used.

It also highlights any conflicts or issues that occurred during the generation process. Sometimes, when you use toggles, select a specific location, or combine certain options, the result may not work exactly as expected.

The summary makes those problems easy to identify. For example, if a main category was selected but one of its sub-options created a conflict, you’ll be able to see exactly where it happened.

That way, you can quickly adjust the settings and refine the next prompt to get something closer to what you actually want.

nodes.title: SAMPLE-SCHEDULER
widgets_values:
I tested it quite a bit, and it behaves pretty much the same way it normally does with the prompter.

Once you start adding multiple styles, LoRAs, or other elements, the results can change depending on the trigger words being used. Make sure any required triggers are also added to the prefix box.

For most of my tests, I used Res_2S, if I do only one pass, because I like the overall look it produces. However, it sometimes gave the images a slight green tint. 2pass, I go EULER-SIMPLE 1ST, EULER-A- SIMPLE 2NDPASS 

For sampling, I mainly used Euler Beta 57 for single , and SIMPLE, FOR DUAL PASS.
I usually kept the steps between 6 and 8, which gave me solid results. Overall, the workflow behaves normally and doesn’t significantly interfere with the generation.

ALL DEPENDS ON WHAT AND HOW MANY LORA YOU USE. HAVE FUN TEST IT, THESE ARE ONLY MY 2 CENTS

I used  the demo with ABBY, but feel free to use any , or none, if you use a character Lora make sure to turn off the " use_character" option. 


put in the prefi box the trigger and a very small description if you want (don over do it ) 

ex: prefix box: brkn_kre2_sophia, Sohpia, 24 years olde beautiful redhead

trigger: brkn_krea2_spphia, Sophia, 

Prefix box can also be used without Lora to make sure some aspec , any class comes out . 
Suffix is the same opposite way , it substract.

required terms
exclude terms works simillarely, use words seperetaed by coma,
